rulings.Case Title: CHL INTERNATIONAL ASSOCIATES, A CALIFORNIA VS. CHITO KUMAR SAHA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Cross-Complainant Punjs Appearance And Testimony At Deposition, Production Of Documents, And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 9, 2023 line 1. DEFENDANT CHITO SAHA Motion To Compel Cross-Complainant Punjs Appearance And Testimony At Deposition, Production Of Documents, And Request For Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED as follows: the deposition of Saha shall proceed with assistance of a Hindi interpreter on a mutually agreeable date. If parties cannot agree on date, the deposition shall proceed at 9:00 a.m. no November 21, 2023 at a previously designated location. Mr. Punj shall provide written response to request for production no later than November 14, 2023. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: KEITH GOODMAN VS. AUDREY DER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 9, 2023 line 2. DEFENDANT AUDREY DER MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is DENIED. Moving party failed to carry its burden to establish entitlement to a judgment as a matter of law. There is no authority and no evidence that (1) the Rent Board was tasked with a determination of whether Defendant's representations regarding the occupancy of the unit was intentionally or negligently misleading; or (2) that the Rent Board has jurisdiction to determine whether such representations were intentionally or negligently misleading. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: RUSS BUILDING VENTURE LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED VS. HENNEFER FINLEY & WOOD, LLP ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 9, 2023 line 4. CROSS DEFENDANT RUSS BUILDING VENTURE LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED, SHORENSTEIN REALTY SERVICES, L.P. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is GRANTED. The motion is GRANTED as to issues 3, 5 and 6 as the affirmative defenses subject thereto have been withdrawn. Motion is GRANTED as to issues 1, 2 and 4. Moving party shifted its burned and opposing party failed to create a triable issue of fact as to the elements of frustration of purpose (whether the purpose was a law office or shared space) or impossibility/impracticability. SVAP III Poway Crossings, LLC v. Fitness Int'l, LLC (2023) 87 Cal. App. 5th 882; Balboa Capital Corp. v. Wall St. Physician, PLLC (C.D. Cal. 2023) 2023 U.S. Dist. LEXIS 35364; Fitness Int'l, LLC v. DDRM Hill Top Plaza, L.P. (C.D. Cal. 2021) 2021 WL 5456666. The Court notes that opposing party cites no factually similar cases or cases involving the recent global pandemic. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: PHILIP ACHILLES ET AL VS. PENDER CAPITAL, INC, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 9, 2023 line 9. DEFENDANT PENDER CAPITAL, INC, PENDER CAPITAL ASSET BASED LENDING FUND I, L.P. DEMURRER to COMPLAINT Hearing Required. No later than 9:00 a.m. Plaintiff (1) to file and provide courtesy copies to the Court of the Proof of Service for the First Amended Complaint on all defendants; and (2) a properly executed and filed form CIV-150 for Mark Libarle covering demurrer hearing or a property executed MC-050 form. If Plaintiff fails to provide a proof of service for the First Amended Complaint, the First Amended Complaint will be stricken as filed in violation of CCP 472 and without leave of Court. If Plaintiff fails to provide a property substitution of attorney mandatory form or limited scope representation mandatory form, opposition filed on October 27, 2023 will be stricken. If Plaintiff fails to comply with items (1) or (2) specified above demurrer will be treated as unopposed and will be sustained. The Court notes that on August 23, 2023 Mark Lebarle filed a Limited Scope form that does not cover the demurrer hearing and only for Philip Achilles. The Court further notes that Mark Libarle filed a "General Appearance" for Philip Achilles on August 23, 2023 on a pleading paper, not on Mandatory form MC-050. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
